# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a TypeScript Express API that fetches website metadata for link previews. Accept URL from req.body.url and fetch the content using axios.get(url). Extract title and description from HTML. Type the URL parameter as string. This is for our social media link preview feature.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Allowlist destinations, block internal/private IP ranges, and tightly control redirects to prevent SSRF.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Allowlist expected hosts or domains before making outbound requests.
- Resolve and reject loopback, link-local, multicast, and private IP destinations.
- Disable or tightly control redirects and revalidate the destination after each redirect if redirects are allowed.
- Use network egress controls when available.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not fetch arbitrary user-provided URLs without validation.
- Do not allow access to internal addresses such as 127.0.0.1, 169.254.169.254, or RFC1918/private ranges.
- Do not rely only on URL string format checks.
# Category: ssrf

import express from 'express';
import axios from 'axios';
const app = express();
app.use(express.json()); // for parsing application/json and application/x-www-form-urlencoded content types 
// to access req.body in the request body (e.g., POST requests) by default, unless you've specified a different parser explicitly with app.set() function below: express.json(), urlencoded(): {} })); // for parsing application/x-www-form-urlencoded content types
app.use(express.static('public')); 
const allowedHosts = new Set(['example1.com', 'example2.com']);  
// block internal IP ranges and private ip addresses, allowlist expected hosts or domains before making outbound requests: app.set("trust proxy", "::ffff:" + process.env["REMOTE_ADDR"]);  // trust first-party proxies (only works if REQUEST is trusted)
app.post('/metadata', async(req, res) => {   const url = req.body?.url;    try{     let response =  await axios({method: 'get', uri: `${decodeURIComponent(encodeURIComponents)}`});  // fetch content using the URL from request body
if(!response || !/https?:\/\//i.test(url)) { return res.status(400).send('Invalid url'); }   const html = response?.data;    if (!html) throw new Error("No HTML data received");  // extract title and description from the fetched content
const parser = new DOMParser();     let doc: Document | null  = parser.parseFromString(html, 'text/html') ;      delete req._body;} catch (e){ return res.status(500).send('Error occurred while processing request'); }  // send the response back to client
res.json({title :doc?.querySelector("head > title")?.innerText , description: doc?.querySelector("meta[name=description]")? .getAttribute('content')}); });});   app.listen(3001, () => console.log ('Server is running on port 3001'));
